client_background:
Vision: Our vision is to create an enduring platform for professional arts journalism that ignites conversation about the arts amongst artists, audiences and the wider community.
To ignite conversation about the arts among artists, audiences and the wider community.

Customers: Our primary customers and users are our readers; and our primary beneficiaries are the Western Australian arts companies, organisations, institutions and artists.
Our stakeholders include our readers, the Western Australian arts industry, and our funding partners - state government, readers, arts companies, private donors and philanthropic trusts/foundations.
Products: Seesaw Magazine – Western Australia’s arts playground – is an independent digital arts magazine founded in August 2017 and co-edited by respected arts journalists Nina Levy and Rosalind Appleby. Drawing on decades of experience in the arts, they lead a team of WA’s most diverse and authoritative arts writers to provide the most dedicated and comprehensive arts coverage in the State.
Our product is coverage of the arts and cultural industry in Western Australia and includes previews, reviews, features, opinion pieces, artist profiles and a monthly podcast about what's on in forthcoming month - our editors' picks of the month.
Our work is trusted and independent.
Competitors: Seesaw Magazine is fulfilling a need in response to the decline of mainstream media outlets such as The West Australian and their diminished, increasingly absent role in covering arts and culture in Western Australia. We are filling a gap in the arts infrastructure in Western Australia. That being the case, we do have competitors. Our recent research has indicated that our competition for arts news is: The West Australian, Local (community newspaper), WA Today, The Australian, ABC Classic FM, RTRFM. Scoop, Artshub, Limelight Magazine. There are also a number of online sites and blogs which were not captured in the research.
Business Model: Our current funds development plan includes the following income sources:
Earned Income from the following streams: advertising (on our website and in our fortnightly e-magazine); monetisation strategies (some yet to be implemented but includes Buymeacoffee)
Philanthropy: individual donors, philanthropic trusts and foundations
Corporate sponsorships
Arts Supporters: Western Australian arts companies, organisations and institutions who make an annual contribution
Government: Currently state government, but investigating Local Government and Federal opportunities.
Challenges: Readership growth! We're a very small start up with limited human and financial resources. Further, given our limited capacity, we have a growing readership but there is low awareness beyond the hard core arts community.
problem_statement: The client seeks to improve their current market position by exploring innovative solutions to emerging challenges in the industry.

client_background:
Vision: Our Vision - is ‘Lives Unaffected by CF’, where every person born with CF will be able to reach their full potential, unencumbered by the disease. Every day we strive a little closer to achieving that vision. 
Our Mission - To provide services to people affected by CF and other respiratory conditions as well as support research, partnering with members, organisations and other stakeholders. 
Customers: People living with Cystic Fibrosis in WA and the support groups. 
Products: Managing the daily treatment of cystic fibrosis (CF) can be quite challenging, whether you are the parent of a newborn, young child, adolescent, or are an adult living with CF. We have a range of support programs to help support you or the person in your life living with CF. Support is available via phone, video call or in the comfort of your own home. 
Home Care Worker Service 
CFWA have a Home Care Worker (HCW) service available in the metropolitan area to provide regular home visits to children and adults with CF. Our HCW program is funded by the Health Department of WA and offers support for airway clearance, nebulisation, exercise, light home duties and occasional respite. Our HCWs receive regular training to provide them with the skills and knowledge they need to supervise treatments safely and effectively. 
Calm Kids, Happy Families Program 
CFWA offer one-on-one support and guidance with our experienced Occupational Therapist (OT), as part of our Calm Kids, Happy Families program. 
Our OT can provide practical solutions and support to the difficulties that can arise in the family system, particularly when also dealing with CF. 
OT's can help you and your child with: 
Developing routines, Calming and de-escalation techniques, Meal and bedtime assistance, Behaviour management techniques, Consistent parenting and Positive communication. 

Building Resilience Program 
Our Youth Social Worker, Haylee, can support young people (~8-11 yrs) through our Building Resilience Program. The one-on-one program uses age-appropriate activities and resources to promote positive mental health, build resilience, address worries and misinformation about CF and encourage independence over CF treatments. 
Getting children used to talking about their feelings at a younger age has positive outcomes for their future mental health. It encourages children to recognise if they are feeling worried and provide them with healthy tools and strategies to process these emotions. 
Hospital Support - Child Support 
Hospital Care Packs and Visits 
We provide Hospital Care Packs to Perth Children’s Hospital (PCH) which are distributed by your CF team as needed. You can ask on the ward if you would like one. 

Financial Support 
We also offer financial assistance for hospital and clinic 
appointments to assist with travel and parking. 
First Hospital Admission 
If your child is admitted to hospital for the first time, we have a ‘My First Admission’ video series which can help you prepare for the admission.

Adult Support - Hospital Food Bag and Visits 
If you’re an inpatient at Sir Charles Gairdner Hospital (SCGH), you can apply online for an Inpatient Food Bag made up of snacks and some toiletry items. 
Financial Support 
We also offer financial assistance for hospital and clinic appointments to assist with travel and parking. 
CFWA Subsidies 
Hospital Parking Subsidy 
CFWA Adult Support Subsidy (16+ years) 
CFWA Family Support Subsidies 
CFWA Equipment 
External Travel Subsidies 
Regional Outreach Program 
Nearly a third of people with cystic fibrosis (CF) in Western Australia (WA) live regionally. Our aim is to bring equitable services and support across the whole of WA. 
Our services team comprise of a social worker/counsellor, 
physiotherapist, community nurse and education officer, which means that we can offer diverse services when we come to your community. 
Regional Subsidies 
Telethon Regional Travel Subsidy 
Adult Regional Travel Subsidy 
Sibling and Offspring Camp Subsidy 
Equipment 
Regional Health Professional Training 
Competitors: None 
Business Model: We have several income sources 
Government contract - 57% 
Donations and Bequests 23% 
Fundraising Events 18% 
Investments 2% 

Challenges: Funding has always been and continues to be our biggest challenge impacting our organisation

client_background:
Vision: 
Inspire a culture of empathy and acceptance 
Mission            
To facilitate an enriching transformative experience through interactive theatre in a safe and nurturing environment 
Customers: Various, corporate, conference bookings, NFP, government. over last 40 years we have worked a significant number of industries and organisations with: 

Community Groups & Organisations that Perth Playback Theatre have worked for include Kalparrin (Parents of children with disabilities) Inc, Baptist Care, Wheatbelt Area Consultative Committee, CLAN WA (Community Link and Network), Gynacological Awareness Week (GAIN Inc), Relationships Australia, Anglicare, The Meeting Place, Belmont Districts Family and Individual Support Association, Community Vision, Multiple Sclerosis Society, Sisters of Mercy, Adult Community Education –ACE and the Youth Involvement Council Hedland.

Government Departments include WA Dept of Planning & Infrastructure, The City of Joondalup, Fremantle Port Authority, Wheatbelt Area Consultative Committee, The Office of Crime Prevention, Legal Aid, City of Cockburn, Attorney Generals Dept., Relationships Australia, North Metro Area Health Services - East, Belmont Districts Family and Individual Support Association, W.A. Dept of Education and Training, Adult Community Education - ACE.

Schools, Colleges and Universities include Prenderville Catholic College, AIM-UWA Business School Alliance, Woodvale Senior High School, Mindarie Senior College, School Industries Leadership Forum, National Educational leaders Conference ACEL & ACE, Saint Simon Peter Catholic Primary School, School Drug Education and Road Aware - DEET, Perth Modern School, Melville Senior High, Hedland Senior High, Catholic Assistant Principals Association, Curtin University of Technology, Leadership Development Unit.

Corporate clients include AIM-UWA Business School Alliance, BHP Billiton, Ausgroup Ltd - AGC Industries Pty Ltd, Fremantle Port Authority, St John of God Healthcare, Integral Development and AHG.
Products: 
Perth Playback Theatre’s work with organisations and communities includes:                                          
 	Interactive performances			  
 	Leadership Team building events                                      
 	Scenario and Role Playing for Human                                
              Resource Development 
 	Facilitating reflection
 	Creating connection
 	Celebrating success
 	Supporting conflict management
 	Supporting change management

Perth Playback Theatre is a form of improvisational theatre, we were able to offer this experience to people, with a story to tell, some with a disability, ATSI and CaLD ethnicity, others in the workplace or at a conference. Support staff who work with community organisations. 

Perth Playback Theatre interprets the audience stories for everyone to feel connected to each other through common experiences, even though they may have been from different countries. Playback theatre is more than just a “re-enactment”, we were able to illuminate the heart of the story, in order to convey the universality for the benefit of the whole audience. 

Competitors: There are only a few similar  identified in WA other states have playback theatre groups too
Business Model: Fee for service, Acts of service ( non - fee paying) workshop fees
Challenges: we are celebrating our 40th year next year, and we have less paid opportunities arising due to Covid and poor marketing

client_background:
Vision: To connect music students and teachers and provide them with quality support & resources online & offline. 
Customers: 1. Music students of all ages and standards (therefore the target can be individuals or families/parents of children wanting to learn) 2. Musicians wanting to be music teachers and music teachers looking for admin support & students 3. Musicians/Music teachers requiring coaching for their music teaching business 4. General public looking to purchase music books, equipment or instruments 
Products: 1.Music lessons in student homes, teacher home studio or online 2. Coaching sessions for Music teachers 3. Over 6000 products in our online shop.
Competitors: 1. Private music teachers ( teaching from their home) & retail music schools 2. Studio Expansion, Tim Topham, teach Music Online etc 3. Music Retailers with online presence
Business Model: 1.Our teachers subscribe to our directory service where we provide software, admin support & student recruitment. Students book through our online booking system for lessons starting with a discounted trial and then pay weekly. We retain approx 40% fees balance paid to teachers. 2. Coaching currently consists of individual 1 hour zoom sessions available in packages. Plan is to release an online course/app in modules. Book was published in 2019. 3 Google Ads drive traffic to online shop. A limited number of products are listed on our facebook pages Wendy's Music school and Music School Success ( Coaching page)
Challenges: Getting quick and cost effective results. Due to the loss of a lot of business from COVID and a move away from retail studio lessons, we need results from any investment to be consistent and fast. As the business is being operated by a sole operator who is also currently teaching full time, marketing would need to be done by a third party, and therefore quoted appropriately.

client_background:
Vision: Our Mission is to help give infants, young children and their families the best possible start in the important, early years of life by improving professional and community understanding that infancy is a critical time for the development of emotional, physical, cognitive, social and mental health. 
Customers: The Community, decision makers, power brokers, parents, professional health, social and child care workers.
Products: Information about Infancy (0-3 yrs) and early childhood, evidence based care promotion. Professional and non-professional training about support and therapy for families, parents and community. Advocacy material.
Competitors: Competition not an issue, if the information presented is compatible. We welcome any group bringing attention to the needs of infants. However, some groups promote information we do not agree with., and believe is unhelpful to families.
Business Model: Association is made up of volunteers and members who pay fees. National Board. State Branches with local committees. Two very part-time paid Project Officers. Training sessions (revenue earning-local and international presenters) promoted through email/distribution lists/word of mouth. National Conferences usually make a small profit. Recently one branch had a bequest made for training nurses in rural and remote locations. Joint ventures with some like minded organisations - cooperating around mutual interests.
Challenges: How to get our message out there. To politicians, decision makers in service providers (advocacy). To parents, to the general community (education health and development  promotion). How to change attitudes that ignores infants and their needs. To promote the understanding that the type of care the first three years of life can set an infant up for their whole future optimal development.

(d)	international projects consistent with the above purpose, such as projects of Rotary International and its constituent entities including The Rotary Foundation and Rotary Australia World Community Service. 
Customers: We primarily operate by funding LEARN ("Lifting Education, Advancing Rural Nepal"), an NGO that we established in Nepal in 2014.  It has four full-time staff committed to training teachers in community (government) schools in remote Western Nepal.  Our programs are planned and conducted in close collaboration with government including the Social Welfare Council, the Center for Education and Human Resource Development (Department of Education) and Rural Municipal Councils.
Products: Training of primary teachers, delivered by masters-degree qualified Nepalese teachers who have the language, local syllabus knowledge and local culture to deliver the training effectively.  Training is delivered to clusters of schools (one or more secondary schools and the feeder primary schools), achieving around 90% voluntary teacher attendance.  Basic programs that deliver 50 days training over three years are followed by annual refresher training and supplemented by the provision of teaching materials.
We have trained more than 500 Nepalese teachers.  Training is currently in abeyance due to the pandemic.
Competitors: We complement rather than compete with organisations like the Australian Himalayan Foundation, NZ and UK Himalayan Trusts which deliver similar services in Eastern Nepal.  We effectively supplement the education funding of the Nepalese government in order to provide training that is desperately needed.
Business Model: Private donations; income from a $1.7 m. capital fund; other income that we can attract from sources such as the relevant Nepalese municipal councils and Rotary International.
Challenges: Succession planning for the 83 year-old Founder, President and operational executive.

client_background:
Vision: Silicon biometrics technology from Phone Pass uses hardware characteristics of electronic devices to uniquely identify them. Silicon Biometrics can improve human biometrics by at least 10 million times and provide a much cost-effective version of machine identity. Our vision is to reduce digital identity fraud by 20% in the first ten years. Currently, Digital identity fraud is growing every single year. In spite of spending USD 170 Billion (source: https://www.fortinet.com/resources/cyberglossary/cybersecurity-statistics) to solve the cybersecurity problem every single year, the loss to global economy every single year is around USD 5 Trillion (source: https://sifted.eu/articles/digital-identity-fraud/ ). Around 69% of all cyberattacks begin with digital identity fraud (source: https://techjury.net/blog/cyber-security-statistics/ ). 
Customers: We would like to partner with the cybersecurity firms providing complementary solution to overcome the Digital Identity fraud problem to tap into the annual USD 44 Billion market size (sources: several – available upon request) and make Phone Pass profitable and start earning revenue. 
Products: Silicon biometrics technology from Phone Pass uses hardware characteristics of electronic devices to uniquely identify them. Silicon Biometrics can improve human biometrics by at least 10 million times and provide a much cost-effective version of machine identity. In lab, we have been able to show one-in-a-billion accuracy for this technology. In some cases, like with accelerometers, the accuracy with six degrees of freedom (three axes and different biases in positive and negative values) and 100 different devices are identified in each degree of freedom as has been observed in the lab, it can lead to one-in-a-trillion accuracy. In that case, the improvement would be 10 billion times.
Competitors: Current gold standard for human identity is human biometrics. The best case of one of the best-known human biometrics – iris scan, has error rate of 0.7%. (The false negative error rate for tests done with single irises, in which a correct match is “missed” by the algorithm, are at 1.5 percent or higher. For two eyes, the rate is .7 percent. Source: https://fcw.com/articles/2012/04/23/nist-iris-recognition.aspx#:~:text=If%20iris%20recognition%20is%20used%20in%20combination%20with,higher.%20For%20two%20eyes%2C%20the%20rate%20is.7%20percent. ) Considering the number of transactions humans perform in the digital space being in billions, if not trillions, this error rate can lead to errors in billions of cases, making it unacceptable.

Gold standard of machine identity is PKI (Public Key Infrastructure). Anyone with experience in PKI can tell you that PKI is expensive to deploy, maintain and run with it being very error prone. The per-user or per-node cost of deploying PKI can be as high USD 74 per user per annum as this article from Verisign shows in addition to one-time setup costs, registration authentication costs, cost of the software, ongoing maintenance and consulting fees. Each of these heads cost circa ten thousand dollars at least when aggregated. Further, some of these line items are ongoing recurring costs often on an annual basis. For a modest IoT network of 100,000 nodes, the total initial cost can run into millions, and yearly recurring expenses will also be in millions.
Business Model: Revenue sharing model with the cybersecurity firms providing complementary solutions to enhance their portfolio of offerings while enhancing their reputation among their customers.
Challenges: Phone Pass has end-to-end proof of concept that demonstrates the technology. However, to be able to run on production systems, there are some hurdles to overcome still, the main one is to make it scalable, based on tools that can support scalability and reliability, and is production grade.

client_background:
Vision: Redeeming the World’s Waste

Mission: IQ Energy Australia is an innovative environmental technologies company focused on repurposing waste beneficially, in local communities and businesses, into clean, renewable, and valuable resources, utilising modular advanced thermal treatment.

About IQ Energy Australia: An Australian start-up company that was founded in 2019 that drew its inspiration from seeing the staggering amounts of waste in low to middle income countries littering the environment and waterways and wanting to find a solution that could incentivise picking this waste up. Partnered with IQ Energy, in Canada, who have significant experience in thermal treatments, such as indirect drying, gasification and pyrolysis, with over 75 years of collective experience designing 25 infrastructure size plants across North America. 

IQ Energy Australia has a focus on dirty and mixed plastic waste, which will be outlined below, as well as sewage treatment and contaminated organics and soils which the technology can be configured to deal with these in a range of ways. By late this year, IQ Energy Australia have two other projects operational, as well as the advanced recycling unit outlined below, these will be an Indirect Dryer unit in Canada and a gasification unit in Northern Regional Victoria.

What we are doing in the plastic space: IQ Energy Australia, with their Canadian partners IQ Energy, are currently developing a modular, scalable, and containerised advanced recycling unit that will recycle dirty and mixed plastics back into a plastic derived crude oil or gas that can be further refined into a variety of consumer or industrial products including virgin plastics. This is the first commercial unit for the company and will be operational in Canada. The technology will utilise its own renewable energy to power and heat the process and the first commercial unit will be operational by early 2022. IQ Energy Australia’s’ vision in developing this technology stemmed from seeing the significant amount of mixed waste in low to middle income countries and recognising the need for a solution that could deal with unsorted waste streams and redeem this plastic waste by putting value on it to stop it being discarded. The purpose is to have a solution that does more than just make waste disappear, but to also have broader positive community impacts.
Customers, users and stakeholders: There is a chain that plastic takes when thinking about our solution. The direct users of the equipment are the waste collectors/waste management companies as they will be the ones bringing in the waste. There is also the petrochemical companies who will be the ones refining the plastic derived crude oil or gas that we produce so that it can get back into the plastic supply chain. There are several other key stakeholders from government, plastic manufacturers and the consumers who will enable and pull through the plastic waste in this solution.
External/competitive environment: There is a wide range of players in the plastic waste space, and we are looking to collaborate with many of them as our focus on dirty and mixed plastic can be quite unique as many solutions are unable to deal with this waste type. Specifically for this project the aim will be to look at other pyrolysis technologies out there that are looking at plastic to oil and back to plastic also known as Advanced Recycling.
Current business model/revenue sources: IQ Energy Australia is looking at business models, such as direct selling, but also licensing as well as subscribing and leasing.
Relevant Sources:
CSIRO Advanced Recycling Blog Article
CSIRO Advanced Recycling Report – We are profiled on Page 25.
Also attached is our IQ Advanced Recycling Brochure if helpful.

client_background:
Vision: Redeeming the World’s Waste

Mission: IQ Energy Australia is an innovative environmental technologies company focused on repurposing waste beneficially, in local communities and businesses, into clean, renewable, and valuable resources, utilising modular advanced thermal treatment.

About IQ Energy Australia: An Australian start-up company that was founded in 2019 that drew its inspiration from seeing the staggering amounts of waste in low to middle income countries littering the environment and waterways and wanting to find a solution that could incentivise picking this waste up. Partnered with IQ Energy, in Canada, who have significant experience in thermal treatments, such as indirect drying, gasification and pyrolysis, with over 75 years of collective experience designing 25 infrastructure size plants across North America. 

IQ Energy Australia has a focus on dirty and mixed plastic waste, which will be outlined below, as well as sewage treatment and contaminated organics and soils which the technology can be configured to deal with these in a range of ways. By late this year, IQ Energy Australia have two other projects operational, as well as the advanced recycling unit outlined below, these will be an Indirect Dryer unit in Canada and a gasification unit in Northern Regional Victoria.

What we are doing in the plastic space: IQ Energy Australia, with their Canadian partners IQ Energy, are currently developing a modular, scalable, and containerised advanced recycling unit that will recycle dirty and mixed plastics back into a plastic derived crude oil or gas that can be further refined into a variety of consumer or industrial products including virgin plastics. This is the first commercial unit for the company and will be operational in Canada. The technology will utilise its own renewable energy to power and heat the process and the first commercial unit will be operational by early 2022. IQ Energy Australia’s’ vision in developing this technology stemmed from seeing the significant amount of mixed waste in low to middle income countries and recognising the need for a solution that could deal with unsorted waste streams and redeem this plastic waste by putting value on it to stop it being discarded. The purpose is to have a solution that does more than just make waste disappear, but to also have broader positive community impacts.
Customers, users and stakeholders: There is a chain that plastic takes when thinking about our solution. The direct users of the equipment are the waste collectors/waste management companies as they will be the ones bringing in the waste. There is also the petrochemical companies who will be the ones refining the plastic derived crude oil or gas that we produce so that it can get back into the plastic supply chain. There are several other key stakeholders from government, plastic manufacturers and the consumers who will enable and pull through the plastic waste in this solution.
External/competitive environment: There is a wide range of players in the plastic waste space, and we are looking to collaborate with many of them as our focus on dirty and mixed plastic can be quite unique as many solutions are unable to deal with this waste type. Specifically for this project the aim will be to look at other pyrolysis technologies out there that are looking at plastic to oil and back to plastic also known as Advanced Recycling.
Current business model/revenue sources: IQ Energy Australia is looking at business models, such as direct selling, but also licensing as well as subscribing and leasing.
Relevant Sources:
CSIRO Advanced Recycling Blog Article
CSIRO Advanced Recycling Report – We are profiled on Page 25.
Also attached is our IQ Advanced Recycling Brochure if helpful.

client_background:
Vision: The TNC vision is to become the "heart" of the Community, providing early intervention strategies  for the disadvantaged and vulnerable, so that all people are empowered to achieve their full potential. To achieve this, TNC's mission is to participate in local planning, and to collaborate and network with other groups to identify the potentially vulnerable and those experiencing crisis, and to network with the community to provide quality programs and services designed to strengthen and promote connectedness and well being throughout our community. 

Mission
We participate in local planning, collaborate and network with other groups to identify the needs within the community and to identify the potentially vulnerable and those experiencing crisis.
We then provide quality programs and services that are often self-funded and specifically target early intervention in the lives of the vulnerable and disadvantaged that are designed to strengthen, build and promote community connectedness, well being and empowerment.
Customers: Children, Young People, Families and Community
Products: Toukley Neighbourhood Centre Inc - 💗 of the community
Toukley Neighbourhood Centre Inc is a registered charity which is funded by NSW Department of Communities and Justice to provide information, referral and support to our local community by walk-in, phone, email, social media or request. The Centre also offers opportunities for community members to apply for the No Interest Loan Scheme (NILS) funded by Department of Fair Trading and also Good Shepherd, this program enables clients to purchase goods and services like fridges, washing machines or car repairs and pay their loan back with no interest. We also run a volunteer scheme funded by Department of Health for Community visitors to visit elderly and socially isolated community members living in aged care facilities. Toukley Neighbourhood Centre also runs 2 social enterprises  in town. Lakes Food Care is a food shop purchasing rescued food from Foodbank NSW & ACT and or TNC Op Shop which provides a small fundraising stream. 
We operate a community centre hub to provide targeted early intervention and support to children, young people, families and disadvantaged and isolated community members with many programs, social groups, activities, courses, workshops, partnership activities and community events. 
Educational groups -  to learn new skills thorough workshops like Artzspace, Better Health Self-Management, Talking Technology or Evidenced Based Parenting classes, like Bringing Up Great Kids. Regular social groups, like supported playgroup, Community Cafe and Youth Yard Drop In, the meeting room and hall hire also provides a space for community groups to meet and connect.
We provide an outreach program, "Pop Up TNC" to our local preschools, schools, shopping centres and outdoor spaces to connect with local children, families and young people in need of support, information and referral.


Competitors: 
Business Model: We are currently funded by 4 streams of government funding for specific program delivery as listed above, NSW Dept. of Communities & Justice, NSW Dept of Fair Trading, Good Shepherd (Federal DSS), Dept of Health (Federal) and also 2 social enterprises. (small Businesses)
Challenges: Human resources/ staff or volunteers with the time and capacity to research funding opportunities, prepare and write submissions to diversify the work we deliver in our community. We never have the time to prepare the comprehensive submissions in the short deadline times. For example we have been trying to prepare a Newcastle Permanent grant for the past 2 years to expand our outreach work using a fitted van as a mobile Neighbourhood Centre.

client_background:
Vision: Spin off from the above parent company to a startup to incubate an MVP in Startspace for the "Great Divide Wholesale Accelerator (GDWA)" (or a better business name to be advised later)

The GDWA startup mission is to primarily wholesale to existing accelerators, tertiary, Centrelink recruitment and other supply labour sources that are willing to provide the millennials and the displaced for the GDWA's SaaS to match-make and accelerate them on specified skills, entrepreneurship and wisdom criteria with the goal to gel at least two generations into cells of up to three persons to build their startups (within industries temporarily frozen by Covid with a high chance of rebooting and to pass on the baton to startups rather than lose all the collective and past expertise to Covid). 

Customers: 1. Displaced people in industries temporarily curtailed by Covid with deep industry know-how but are not tech savvy.
2. Tech savvy millennials who aspire to be entrepreneurs but have no deep domain knowledge of the above industries.
3. Government/non-profit organisations, recruitment agencies including tertiary wanting to help both the young and displaced older people, irrespective of any other differences

Addressing their imbalance problem:

Mismatch of the digital, social media and tech skills already in abundant possession by the younger generation and their notable absence in the older Covid displaced generation who have deep industry expertise which the former lacks, i.e. the tech and wisdom divide.

Products: Crowdsource from the affected stakeholders, entrepreneurs and the larger concern community to fund and develop a proprietary SaaS matchmaking platform - to digitally  match-make and accelerate them on specified skills, entrepreneurship and wisdom criteria and curate them into startup cells of up to three persons per cell to build their startups - and accelerator until the GDWA can sustain itself with a subscription model that charges the successful startups, retail accelerators, tertiary institutions and other actors benefitting from the GDWA SaaS (Eco-system approach).

Some Key Metrics:
1)	No of matchmaking cells per quarter 
2)	No of breakups per quarter
3)	2 divided by 1 < 10%
4)	No. of successful startups/year raising funds
5)    No. of participants joining per quarter

Competitors: A non-profit and crowdsourcing (initially) SaaS model to match-make at least two generations of entrepreneurs in the disruptive Covid time is untested and too hard to even try to do, hence it is unknown on the number of competitors.  However, I have researched the foundation based on an Israel University model that attempted to help more mature people there build startups in their later life.  I am now requesting Practera's assistance with their University students to "brainstorm" the feasibility of the GDWA through more research.

Business Model: Initially, crowdsource, then a combination of it and subscriptions, grants and Philanthropy.

Challenges: The MVP is still a work-in-progress with a high failure rate similar to any other startups.  However, having been in small business for more than 25 years, and with the backing of our existing company, and experienced a number of product and entrepreneurial life cycles, I may be able to mitigate the risks.

Competitors: A non-profit and crowdsourcing (initially) SaaS model to match-make at least two generations of entrepreneurs in the disruptive Covid time is untested and too hard to even try to do, hence it is unknown on the number of competitors. I am now requesting Practera's assistance with their University students to "brainstorm" the feasibility of the GDWA through more research.

Business Model: Initially, crowdsource, then a combination of it and subscriptions, grants and Philanthropy.

However, having been in small business for more than 25 years, and with the backing of our existing company, and experienced a number of product and entrepreneurial life cycles, I may be able to mitigate the risks.

client_background:
We have within our product range a Pulp Density Gauge that measures Specific Gravity and Pulp Density, a Balance Scale.
It is a good-sized market and there are only two players, ourselves and another company located in the USA.
Our product is remarkably similar to each other’s.
Both products are a manually operated scale, they have springs, a dial like a clock. Considerably basic, but now they are all that is available. Digital scales are not offered with the versatility of the manual scales. Price is also in the thousands, where we are both prices in the order of $400 per set.
The scales are sold worldwide, every mining operation, mineral processing plant, every mineral laboratory uses scales. We would average 500 sales units per annum, our competitor would average 5000. All this in the mining industry alone.
It should be noted that the competitor has brand recognition far greater than us, Clients regularly refer to the Scale (ours of the competitors) as a Marcy Scale (Marcy being the competitor’s brand) Similar to the use of the word “Sellotape” customers generally don’t want the Brand “Sellotape” just a sticky tape. Same for the use of “Google” when asked where to find information, people say “Just Google it” be it Google, Bing or any other search engine.

We have been working on a digital model and are close to being able to bring it to the market. It has significant advantages over the manual model.
Does not need calibration.
Program upgrades are a simple operation.
Far greater range for SG and pulp density calculation.
Manual model works from an SG of 1.00 to 7.80.
Digital works between 0.001 – 99.99
This sees a far greater customer base, for example, the petroleum industry works in the SG range of 0.80 – 0.96. there is nothing freely available for this industry.
Costs are exceptionally low for the manufacturer and given the advantages, this presents a good opportunity to increase margins significantly.
Competitor Balance Scale - Marcy Pulp Density/Specific Gravity Scale | Mechanical Scales for Material Testing and Scales Testing Equipment - Certified Material Testing Products (certifiedmtp.com)
Our Balance Scale - Clyde & Marcy Pulp Density Scales (cmae.com.au)
problem_statement: The client seeks to improve their current market position by exploring innovative solutions to emerging challenges in the industry.
project_scope:

At present we only have the working bits of the scale, not the case to hold it all together, - what options should we look at?
Where can we expand our market to? Clients, Countries, Industries?
Develop a marketing strategy, given the restraints of travel in this COVID world.
Distributors
Agents
Direct Sales
Price point
What is a sustainable price level that the industry will accept for the Digital Scale in preference to the manual scale?
Do we need IP or Copyright protection?
Level of cover
Area of cover (Countries)
Associated costs.
How do we compensate our product developers for their work? This work has currently been on a voluntary basis by two friends of mine.
Grants
Do we look at R & D grants?
Other product development grants or assistance.

Supply Chain
Look at suitable supply chains for our product
International sales
Domestic Sales
Packaging/ transport issues for the safe delivery of scientific equipment.
How do we ensure our supply chain is suitably geared up for the delivery to our clients/dealers/agents? (Note that the Mining customers are generally in remote locations with poor roads, bulk transport options with road trains, and poor operating conditions)
Finance
What would be the finance requirements for the initial product build-up
Tooling requirements
Possible minimum order quantities for specific parts
Assembly
Initial Stock Holding
Sources of funding
Return on investment strategies
Strategies
Marketing
How do we market the product to the various industries the original program identified?
Dairy Industry
Mining Industry
Via what distribution channels.
Marketing initiatives
Print
Web Based
Social Media
Again, our mining clients are in remote areas, it is very difficult and costly to go to site and market our product, given the price may only be $1,000 per piece. Our larger clients may only have 2-3 units on site.
other_notes:
With the new program having expanded disciplines encompassing Supply Chain, Finance and Marketing, we now have the opportunity to add valuable content to the strategies already completed by our initial program through Uni SA.

We hold community workshops which provide opportunities for people with a disability to engage in dance and theatre in a respectful, supportive and inclusive setting around the Northern Rivers region. The Sprung!! the dance ensemble has developed 4 new full-length works, two of which have been finalists for 2017 and 2018 Australian Dance Awards for outstanding community dance. We engage with the wider community through partnerships, workshops and performances bringing guest artists and tutors to the region to build the skills of local arts workers and creatives.

Sprung!! earns money through engagements to hold workshops in disability arts for local councils and arts councils. We raise philanthropic funds through campaigns and fundraising activities.

We are looking for the Student Consulting Team to research the following:

The blue pointers have been covered by a previous student team. Please use their research report as a base for you to work on the following deliverables that would allow expansion to include the additional disciplines.
We would like to have a team work on all aspects of this product, 
At present we only have the working bits of the scale, not the case to hold it all together, - what options should we look at?
Where can we expand our market to? Clients, Countries, Industries?
Develop a marketing strategy, given the restraints of travel in this COVID world.
Distributors
Agents
Direct Sales
Price point
What is a sustainable price level that the industry will accept for the Digital Scale in preference to the manual scale?
Do we need IP or Copyright protection?
Level of cover
Area of cover (Countries)
Associated costs.
How do we compensate our product developers for their work? This work has currently been on a voluntary basis by two friends of mine.
Grants
Do we look at R & D grants?
Other product development grants or assistance.

Supply Chain
Look at suitable supply chains for our product
International sales
Domestic Sales
Packaging/ transport issues for the safe delivery of scientific equipment.
How do we ensure our supply chain is suitably geared up for the delivery to our clients/dealers/agents? (Note that the Mining customers are generally in remote locations with poor roads, bulk transport options with road trains, and poor operating conditions)
Finance
What would be the finance requirements for the initial product build-up
Tooling requirements
Possible minimum order quantities for specific parts
Assembly
Initial Stock Holding
Sources of funding
Return on investment strategies
Strategies
Marketing
How do we market the product to the various industries the original program identified?
Dairy Industry
Mining Industry
Via what distribution channels.
Marketing initiatives
Print
Web Based
Social Media
Again, our mining clients are in remote areas, it is very difficult and costly to go to site and market our product, given the price may only be $1,000 per piece. Our larger clients may only have 2-3 units on site.
other_notes:
With the new program having expanded disciplines encompassing Supply Chain, Finance and Marketing, we now have the opportunity to add valuable content to the strategies already completed by our initial program through Uni SA.
I encourage students to add additional content to the program as they see fit.
From previous programs I have worked on, I have found that it is critically important that all discipline teams communicate closely with each other team to ensure conflicting findings or recommendations are not conveyed to the client, all disciplines must have consensus on the initial and final reports. These programs are “team projects” covering all disciplines applied to the project
deliverables:
An oral presentation of the completed project.
A report summarizing your findings.

client_background:
Vision: Vision: To create a more enjoyable travel experience with a simple, yet effective lock for every backpacker.
Mission: To add security and peace of mind in every travel with a simple, affordable, and light-weight lock for every backpacker.
 
Customers: Buckle Lock is a travel accessory for backpackers, designed specifically to secure side release buckles found on many top-loading backpacks. It can also be used to secure buckles on other types of bags, such as camera bags, and cycle bags. 
Products: Buckle Lock is a lock to secure a backpack. Backpacks come in two kinds – with zips, or with side release buckles. Backpack with zips are easy to secure as you can use any padlock in the market. That is not the case for side release buckles that are found on many top-loading backpacks. The unique design of a buckle means that you cannot use a padlock to secure it. This leaves a lot of backpackers vulnerable to theft. Buckle Lock (patent pending) is designed to secure the side release buckles on a top-loading backpack, thereby stopping a thief from entering the backpack and stealing valuable belongings.
Competitors: Our main competition is Pacsafe. They sell a lot of travel security related products. One product in particular, backpack wire mesh, covers the entire backpack. The other competitor is Osprey Airporter (Deuter also have a similar product), which is essentially a duffel bag inside which the backpack is stored, and the zips of the duffel bag are locked.

There used to be a similar product to our’s in the market before, Pacsafe BuckleSafe. This product is discontinued.
Business Model: The business is currently in pre-revenue stage. The business started just before the pandemic and was put on hold during the pandemic. 
Challenges: We are looking for the Student Consulting Team to complete an analysis into IP protection for our organisation in particular: 
●	Recommendations to protect the IP of my product
●	Provide recommendations on how to stop product copy cats
●	Patent searches for any prior art similar to my product
●	Does my product breach any existing patents
●	Any other recommendations in relation to IP protection

client_background:
Vision: The Association was founded in 1935. For over 85 years we have gathered wonderful stories of the lives of the early pioneers of South Australia. We have an extensive historical collection, in multiple formats, of the early years and we aim to preserve and share  this unique history for future generations.
Membership to the Association is open to any person whose ancestor arrived in the colony, now the State of South Australia, on or before 28 December, 1846.  
Customers: Everyone who has an ancestor(s) who arrived in South Australia before December 28, 1846. 
Products: The Pioneers Association of SA Inc is a non-profit incorporated organization, entirely managed by volunteers. We offer a range of activities which focus on South Australian history and collect and produce publications to further knowledge and interest. 
Competitors: We exist in a niche market, but we are seeking ways to increase and retain members in competition with other community, state and national historical bodies. 
Business Model: We are a voluntary organisation reliant on willingness of members to support our endeavours. Our principal revenue source is our annual membership subscriptions & joining fee for new members. 
Challenges: Need to improve technological understandings and skills of older volunteers to be able to utilize current social media and digital platforms to communicate with members, community and encourage potential members. How do we engage potential members unable to attend functions, and give them value for money? What will encourage the younger demographic to join?

client_background:
Vision: Gosford Regional Community Services Inc. (GRCS) is a not for profit, welfare and benevolent organization whose principal purpose is to advance social or public welfare and to relieve the effects of poverty. In order to fulfil this purpose the organisation undertakes a range of activities including, but not limited to:
•	Delivering flexible support to children, young people, families and communities experiencing, or at risk of, vulnerability. 
•	Providing beneficial assistance to those in need including material aid and emergency financial relief to those experiencing financial hardship for any reason, including natural disasters.
•	Providing opportunities for people with a physical or mental disability to participate equally in community.
•	Establishing and operating child care facilities to provide care for school aged children before and after school hours and during school holidays.
•	Providing approved services under the national disability insurance scheme.
•	Provide community information, education and advocacy, community activities and a variety of other support services to empower people to deal with issues of importance to themselves and the community
 
Customers: Customers/Users:  Any member of the community. Stakeholders: Members, Government Agencies (Federal, State and Local), Philanthropic Organisations; Funding Partners, Schools,

Competitors: Other NGOs operating in the same space. Large NGOs present the greatest competition.
Business Model: Approx 30% from Government Funding and Grants; 70% Social Enterprise (mainly childcare)
Challenges: Large NGOs and changing funding landscape.

client_background:
Vision: THE REALM OF POSSIBILITY PROJECT – OUR DECLARATION
A PROJECT COMMITTED TO EXPLORING AND FOSTERING AN UNDERSTANDING OF ‘THE REALM OF POSSIBILITY’ IN CREATING A SUSTAINABLE WORLD

 

‘Few would deny humanity faces existential threats.

Our world is in an unsafe state – environmentally, economically, sociologically.

Unintended consequences from our technologically advanced society are contributing to a future for all sentient beings and for nature that is not assured.

The problems we face are systemic.

These threats notwithstanding, humanity has the collective power to bequeath to our children and future generations a sustainable, stable and life-supporting world.

It is clear that our current ways of redressing those threats are inadequate.

We need a systemic approach – and this document puts forward such an approach.

The Realm of Possibility Project’s vision is to foster and support a systemic, enlightened approach to achieving a sustainable future.

This declaration sets out a direction and an approach with which we hope you, upon investigation, will want to engage.

It is an approach whereby individuals, families, private- and public-sector organisations and governments recognise how each of us, individually and collectively, either create and sustain ourselves and our environment, or work against that stable, sustainable state.

Humanity is faced with a clear choice:

1) continue along the low-road that we are on, or

2) wake up, reset and take the high road – i.e. being in service to the common good and redressing what ails the world.’

– John Wood, 2014

OUR VISION PURPOSE AND VALUES
VISION
We see a future in which our children and future generations live in an environmentally, economically and socially life-supporting world; one where kindness, understanding, wisdom and common sense prevail.

PURPOSE
To fulfill our VISION by facilitating and fostering community understanding of what is behind the simple words ‘Possibility’ and ‘impossibility’, and how these two states of mind determine how we see reality and create our world.

VALUES
Each of us independently will pursue our collective PURPOSE around the sustainability of our total environment by looking in the direction of our innate Realm of Possibility, embracing the spirit of kindness, understanding, wisdom and common sense that imbues our thinking, feelings and actions upon seeing into that reality.

PHILOSOPHY AND RATIONALE
The Realm of Possibility Project is a non-partisan, apolitical, not-for-profit, charitable Foundation, offering tax deductibility for donations made within Australia.

We offer a service to individuals and organisations of all political convictions, ethnicities, religions, sexual orientations and philosophical views: to redress what ails society via the quality of human thinking and our use of Thought. That is the genesis of what ails humanity in every aspect of our lives – environmentally, economically and socially.

The evidence of widespread environmental devastation, economic imbalances, and social ill-will addiction, hatred, violence and war tells us clearly that humanity is not at peace with itself. We are misusing the gift that makes us human: our unique capacity to think.

Transforming the prevailing trend is possible with widespread, concerted, ongoing facilitation in seeing a fresh way forward.

With our limited understanding of how we create our personal reality, and therefore our world, we add daily to the sum of human distress and, potentially, to the destruction of our habitats and societies.

It is our individual and collective lack of awareness that threatens the sustainability of our planet and thus our lives. That deficiency is global, currently unyielding, and seemingly increasing.

Looking within, we come to understand what living our life from Possibility or impossibility means to us, our families, neighbours, communities and nations. As a result, we see with fresh eyes our lives and our relationships – including with the planet, all sentient beings and the totality of nature.

MISSION
Our MISSION is three-fold:

To demonstrate the relationship between the quality of our thinking, our attitudes and therefore our behaviour towards our natural environment and our fellow sentient beings.
To encourage each other in this understanding and in taking personal responsibility and accountability for the state of our relationship with ourselves; our families; our organisations – private and public; our community; other nations; and, critically, with the natural world.
To assist and encourage others in seeing Possibility, in contrast to seeing only impossibility, and thereby understand how we, you and I, transform our relationships for the benefit of the common good and the sustainability of our planet.
OUR AIMS
In honouring our DECLARATION, The Realm of Possibility Project will provide a rallying point, be a credible resource and multi-faceted communications centre for seeing Possibility and the outcomes achievable from that seeing.

Our key aim is to point in the direction of Possibility and thus positively influence the community on:

The cause of humankind’s dysfunction – our poor use of the gift of Thought – with too many of us living in the effect of that, i.e. our impossibility thinking, feelings and behaviour toward each other and the planet.
The solution to humankind’s dysfunction and the state of the planetary environment – i.e. our understanding of Thought. Therefore, we aim to help people live more of their lives influenced by the realm of Possibility and the inherent kindness, understanding, wisdom and common sense guiding our thinking, feelings and behaviour toward each other and the environment.
To achieve this end, we recognise certain elements critical to The Realm of Possibility Project’s success or failure. The Project will be and remain relevant only if:

we provide quality materials that point clearly in the direction of our innate wholeness – kindness, understanding, wisdom and common sense.
our retreats and training programs deliver to participants an experience:
that enables them to see Possibility and therefore experience the wholeness inherent in each of us
that shows how we innocently live unconsciously from our accumulation – our lifelong beliefs, opinions and judgements, believing them to be ‘the truth’; thus we live from impossibility.
We grow our membership and retain and foster our members’ involvement and support.
we keep foremost in mind that our sustainability lies in the integrity and authenticity we bring to our PURPOSE, AIM, VISION AND VALUES; and reviewing and revising as necessary our MISSION, OUTCOMES AND MEANS.
WHAT WE STAND FOR
The Realm of Possibility Project is created by concerned, committed and motivated human beings in service to a sustainable world.

People who recognise that understanding the source (for good or ill) of all human experience is Thought and the quality of our thinking.

People who see that understanding the nature of Thought and thinking is essential if we are to witness humanity at peace with itself and as the protective custodians of our natural environment.

Our single focus is to show how we can experience our innate human qualities of kindness, understanding, wisdom and common sense – qualities we all share at our most fundamental level when experiencing the origins of Thought – in that space we call The Realm of Possibility.

Moreover, in experiencing fresh thought, we foster and support the community's will to redress the social, societal, economic and environmental problems facing humanity and the planet.

The Realm of Possibility Project recognises that humanity confronts vast challenges in sustaining itself. These include, but are not limited to:

Substantial environmental threats to the planet: our flora, fauna, oceans, rivers, rainforests, farmlands, the atmosphere, et al,
increasing population levels,
escalating social inequality and its devastating consequences,
economic chaos, from decades of poor decisions on a wide range of governance issues at the sovereign, corporate and personal level.
We assert that each of the above problems is a symptom of our individual and collective lack of awareness. Each problem humanity faces is a direct consequence of our lack of understanding that our misuse of Thought, individually and as a collective, is at cause in the perilous state in which we find ourselves.

NOTHING CAN TAKE PRECEDENCE OVER THE REALITY THAT WE, AS A COLLECTIVE, ARE AT CAUSE IN BOTH THE PROBLEMS WE FACE AND, EQUALLY, IN THE SOURCE OF THE SOLUTIONS TO WHAT AILS US.

We welcome the support of people who see beyond the limitations and ideological constraints of politics and all forms of coercive power and control to be a part of this project. And we understand that we must work constructively within the political systems.

Our sole motivation is the sustainability of planet Earth, the natural world and all sentient beings.

We cannot be plainer. Our very function is to dispel the myths that blind us and, with kindness, understanding, wisdom and common sense, to counter those myths with the reality that we are both the cause of the problems and the source of the solutions. And to point to how we can contribute to the solutions in an engaging, inspiring way that taps the essence of who we are as human beings.

In summary, we understand that people:

who become informed and engaged in seeing the cause of the prevailing reality of our own and, as a consequence, of global unsustainability and,
who, as a result of that engagement, experience a change of mind and therefore heart,
(that is, their worldview around what is at cause in creating this parlous state environmentally, economically and socially, and what they need to do),
will themselves transform and, with their fresh thinking and new behaviours, will become part of the solution, helping to create the massive planetary transformation necessary.

ASSISTING, FACILITATING, ENCOURAGING AND SHOWING THOSE INTERESTED INDIVIDUALS A WAY FORWARD IN CREATING CHANGE IS THE REALM OF POSSIBILITY PROJECT’S JOB. IT’S OUR ONLY JOB.

HOW WE VIEW THE NEXUS THAT EXISTS BETWEEN SEEING POSSIBILITY AND SEEING IMPOSSIBILITY
As The Realm of Possibility Project moves forward, we must strive to express ourselves in ways that make sense to those we speak with regarding the validity, potential and efficacy of our proposition. Essentially, we operate as human beings from either a state of mind that is seeing or being influenced by having seen Possibility, or from a state of mind that is seeing impossibility.

The quality of our relationship with ourselves, with our life partners, with our family, friends, colleagues, neighbours and the rest of the world reflects that state – one of Possibility or one of impossibility.

We recognise that our state of mind is often poor – we see life from a state of impossibility. That is the beginning, the middle and the end to what ails the world.

To prosecute our case, we need to demonstrate that the remedy for what ails each of us is to see and be influenced by a state of Possibility most of the time. That is the task we face.

Moreover, we strive to identify the extent to which various broad groupings of people see and respond to the idea that we are the source of our problems via our erroneous thinking, and how they might think about and respond to our messages.

In the broadest possible terms, we have identified the following four groupings.

Keep in mind as you read the following four categories that each of us can be accurately categorised as doing the best we can see to do; waking up to seeing Possibility is the transformative element in moving forward.

Group A – AWAKE & THUS COMMITTED

These individuals acknowledge to a significant degree their own 100% contribution to the parlous state of the world. These individuals join the dots between their state of mind and a sustainable natural environment, a sustainable economic environment and a sustainable societal environment.

This grouping of individuals are living their lives within their community in a more conscious way, and are kind, understanding people who live life with a high level of wisdom and common sense.

Group A people demonstrate in their lives how to live in a stable, sustainable, giving and loving world.

This group is intrinsically motivated to protect the natural environment.

Group B – AWAKE & COMMITTED TO VARYING DEGREES

These individuals acknowledge to a limited degree their contribution to what ails themselves and the world. Their understanding of and attitudes toward human thinking being at cause in the problem the world faces varies widely.

They have concerns about the existential threats we face, such as climate change, the disparity between the haves and have-nots, and the breakdown in social cohesion throughout the world. But again, to varying degrees, these individuals currently feel lost, believing that anything they do doesn’t count in the grand scheme of things. Therefore, they are not drivers of kindness, understanding, wisdom and common sense within their communities in addressing our pressing environmental, economic and social problems.

Unlike Group A, these people are more likely to focus on personal, tangible concerns and priorities. These include job security, the economy, health, education and anything which appears to be more pressing and immediate than creating a sustainable world at peace with itself.

This Group, to a degree, is motivated intrinsically around caring for others and the natural environment, but their extrinsic values and personal needs and more materialistic lifestyles hold sway in their lives.

Group C – ASLEEP AND COMMITTED TO THEIR OWN BELIEFS, OPINIONS, JUDGEMENTS AND CIRCUMSTANCES

These individuals point at ‘them out there’ and to external problems as to what ails them and the world.

These people currently represent the largest part of the population. Variously, they may feel confused or lack interest in understanding the state of their own minds and therefore the state of the world they are creating.

They see the world coming at them, not being created by them.

This group is driven by its intrinsic need for survival – food and shelter and extrinsic values such as having enough money. These folks don’t recognise that the very things they need and value are at risk due to the existential threats to the stability and sustainability of the world.

Group D (Mixed Group) – THE DENIERS, IDEOLOGUES, MONEY- AND POWER-DRIVEN

These individuals also point to external problems as to what ails themselves and the world. These people represent a very influential, often financially and politically powerful section of the population, and are driven very strongly by their extrinsic values.

In the main, this group deny their personal 100% contribution to the world we create. Their heavy conditioning blinds them to seeing that we, not ‘them’, are at cause in the world we create.

Members of this group are often active in disputation, seeking power and control. Their level of activism may confine itself to the ballot box but frequently extends its influence to sectarian and other forms of violence. This group, because of its entrenched views, extrinsic values, is the hardest to reach.

FOR ALL OF US, YOU AND I, FALLING INTO THIS LAST GROUP IS THE BIGGEST THREAT WE FACE. WE MUST AVOID ‘SMOKING OUR OWN DOPE’ AND CONTINUE TO LOOK IN THE DIRECTION OF OUR INTRINSIC VALUES – KINDNESS, UNDERSTANDING, WISDOM AND COMMON SENSE: THE REALM OF POSSIBILITY AND SEEING AFRESH.

CHANGING ATTITUDES
SUSTAINED, RELEVANT, ENGAGING, FACILITATIVE COMMUNICATION will create openness.

The Realm of Possibility Project’s primary task is to facilitate transformation in people’s awareness so:

there is a migration from Group B to A, and from Group C into B
Possibility is seen by the most powerful and intrangient group, and a migration from D to C occurs
The more people who migrate into a more intrinsically motivated group – including politicians and other key spheres of influence such as industry, commerce and media – the greater will be the impact on our world.

Group A

Since these people are very concerned and willing, they form most of our initial support base either directly or indirectly.

Group B

These are our secondary target audience. When these Australians are effectively communicated with, influenced at the level of their intrinsic values and shown a way forward, large numbers will support the same personal and collective outcomes around self-awakening as Group A.

Once Group A increases in number, it will be a voting force that no political party of any persuasion can ignore. Indeed, politicians of all persuasions will seek the endorsement of these people.

(The Realm of Possibility Project contends that the critical mass and momentum to produce some change is upward of 20% of the population. For a significant change, critical mass is upward of 30%. Massive change requires upward of 40%. That said, as we materially influence a real and sustainable awakening, it is eminently possible that a further 5 to 15% will progressively grasp the significance of the inside-out nature of the human experience and create the community awakening needed).

Group C

Our outcome is to adopt fresh, powerful values-based communications to redress uninformed scepticism, lack of interest, and apathy. If we maintain our focus and energy to inform Group C, breathe life and engagement into our conversation with them and reinforce the links between inaction, and personal and family consequences in a way that encourages transformation rather than frightens people, there will be migration into Group B.

Group D

This group, like most of us a lot of the time, are certain they are right and others are wrong. We are not targeting this group, but need to respectfully acknowledge their beliefs, opinions and judgements without insisting on our own position. If we fall into that trap, we ourselves are firmly in Group D.

THE CHALLENGES IN SUMMARY
The current state of the world suggests too few people understand or appreciate the magnitude and immediacy of the threat that we, the people, pose to life on Earth.
Similarly, too few fully comprehend that our contribution via our seeing Possibility or seeing impossibility is the source of the threat we pose.
Knowledge of the availability of the simple but profound understanding we point to is almost non-existent, and its very simplicity in the face the prevailing world view – the beliefs, opinions and judgement – is its greatest virtue and perhaps its greatest challenge.
General community apathy is high. In this psychological environment, cynicism and inaction prevail. Without a shift in community understanding, and thus perception and attitude, the course humanity is on is one of self-destruction.
The world is hostage to our fears, ignorance and thus the society of expediency we have created.
Funding, volunteer skills, and awakened and thus motivated people are currently in short supply. Conveying THE REALM OF POSSIBILITY’S message rests largely on the extent to which we can energise and augment this existing small force.
Summing up: We will encounter people who are certain that what we point toward is a dubious concept at best, with questionable foundations, and therefore is not to be taken seriously.
A FINAL WORD
We recognise that THE REALM OF POSSIBILITY PROJECT’S intentions are bold. Some will say unrealistic, others a mission impossible.

We say in response: with a kind and consistent approach, we can and will, as a group looking in the direction of Possibility, halt the drift toward impossibility thinking – thinking which, in observing social media, watching the nightly news or reading the daily newspapers, is endemic.

Only by individual human insight into the realm of Possibility, sustained by a sufficient number of us over the long term, can we build community will to ultimately overcome the impossibility thinking that plagues the world and is at the heart of each problem humanity faces.

Without that action, our life-supporting ecological environment will collapse. We will face the devastation of our life-supporting arable land, precious waterways, clean air and, as a result, all sentient beings.

We must stop relying on our politicians to take the lead. We must show them the way forward.

The implications are clear if we do nothing. It’s not enough to be witness to disaster, and nor is it necessary. There is urgent work to be done by us all.

THE REALM OF POSSIBILITY PROJECT welcomes your support and involvement in whichever way/s you’re able to contribute to creating community understanding and thus a kinder, wiser populous and a more peaceful and sustainable world.

client_background:
Vision: Vision: To grow the capacity of Australians to support
each other when we need it most.

Mission:To collaboratively create mentally healthy workplaces
and communities, who are able to provide initial help for suicide prevention and better mental health journeys.  
Customers: Customers: primarily corporate organisations. Users are the participants of the workshops. Stakeholders are the participants of the courses and all other employees of the organisation as well, given that the reason for rolling out the training is usually to try to manage WHS risks and to have people within the organisation who can provide initial support to peers within the organisation. I as the sole trader/business owner/founder am a stakeholder. I also have other Instructors that I hire if/when required to deliver workshops under my banner. 
Products: Currently, I mainly deliver accredited workshops (MHFA First Aid mostly) with additional components such as periodic follow-up/refresher sessions. I deliver primarily to organisations creating mentally healthy workplaces. Other services that I am starting to offer are shorter online facilitated training for SMEs covering various topics related to mental health awareness and self-care. These are currently being funded by the local council but could potentially be delivered B2B. Although I can and do deliver workshops to the public, I am primarily a Corporate Trainer.
Competitors: My competitors include everything from Sole-Traders such as myself, that provide primarily the same (MHFA) and/or similar training right through to large organisations/NGOs, for example Black Dog Institute and Beyond Blue provide free training on mental health related topics and free resources etc., as well as other services. So a workplace may choose to simply rely on the free offerings of those large organisations rather than look to a small provider such as myself. Having said that, people often find out about the courses I offer due to starting out on the websites of those larger organisations (not about my business personally as they don't advertise businesses such as mine, but the programs such as Mental Health First Aid. As my business is still in it's early days my main source of income is as a freelance contractor/sub-contractor which ironically means that I am often contracted by some of my competitors. For example, I deliver online courses for Wesley Mission, some Lifeline Centres,  and Mentally Well Workplaces who are based in QLD. I can provide a list of a number of competitors and some of our points of difference and points of similarities.
Business Model: Current revenue sources  
1. freelance contractor/sub-contract as an Instructor for various organisations. Delivery modes include virtual conferencing via Zoom or in-person (when not restricted by lockdown/restrictions)  
2. deliver workshops to my own corporate clients (for my own business, Mental Health Training Essentials). Delivery modes include virtual conferencing via Zoom or in-person (when not restricted by lockdown/restrictions) 
Challenges: Being a sole trader and working on the business and in the business

Customers, users and stakeholders – Sydney community, often referred through caseworkers.

client_background:
GYGC’s vision is to help more people achieve success as gaming YouTubers.  Our customers definitions of success seem to differ from person to person: Some wish to build a community of like-minded people and expand their social life online, some simply have a passion for content creation and creating gaming videos is a great outlet for them, some love the idea of entertaining people and/or being a public role model, and some want to quit their corporate jobs to become full time YouTubers (We understand these personas are vague, but this is precisely why we are interested in market research!).  Regardless of the dream, growing a successful gaming YouTube channel helps these people live meaningful and fulfilling lives.  To date, our small team at GYGC has helped hundreds of thousands of people in their journey to becoming successful YouTubers - we’ve even helped people quit their jobs/drop out of college to become full time YouTubers.  You can find a few student video testimonials on this landing page, if you’re interested https://growyourgamingchannel.com/
Our primary revenue streams as of late are from our online products.  We sell digital products and educational courses (Such as the one linked above) internationally.  These products are usually between the $100-$500 mark.  The majority of our customers seem to reside in the US, UK and Australia.  There is also a large market for our products in India, but we have discovered that the Indian market is less likely to be able to afford our products.So far, while we have created a number of online education products since COVID19 started, we feel as though none of our online courses have achieved product market fit.  Our current courses just haven’t “clicked” with a large enough segment of the market - which is understandable, since our courses were created based on our assumptions of what we thought the market would want, instead of objective market research.  So, we would like to go back to the drawing board and start again by identifying what the market really wants, then reverse engineering a new course based on what the market really wants. We would greatly appreciate your help with the market research part of this initiative.  
We also recently conducted a market research survey (880 survey responses) which may reveal some valuable findings if analysed by skilled individuals.  If you have data scientists on your team, we’re particularly excited to see if some in-depth regression or meta analysis (or something similar?) will reveal valuable correlations/insights. https://drive.google.com/file/d/1P60o_VUnPVaTM7vIrY9SmfJkd1ReDovF/view?usp=sharing 
Any market research will be valuable to us, but it’d be awesome if - by the end of our working relationship - we could have identified some specific segments/personas of the market that the GYGC team could then create a scalable and profitable online course for.  (Oversimplified example: “the data indicates that you should create a product for 30+ year old males who want to quit their jobs and become full time YouTubers.  We estimate that there are approx. 50,000 of these individuals world wide”, OR “based on our analysis of your market and the survey you provided, we think you should create a course for 18-24 year old college students that will teach them how to get their first 1,000 subscribers.  We estimate that there are around 150,000 of these individuals world wide”).  Bonus points if we can identify specific advertising channels through which we can target these segments.

Customers: 986,479 Australians participated in golf in 2019 over 1500 golf courses and $985 million spent 
We have the opportunity to tap into the $1.5 billion-dollar global golf tee market. We are recognised by eight national wholesalers and have active involvement with Walkerden Golf Wholesalers
We are charity partners for the upcoming Pennant Hills Golf Day with Waitara BMW. Eastwood Golf Course actively support and promoting The Naiteev™ Golf Tee. In 12 months we aim to expand to another 5% of the 600 pro shops in Australia
Webb Martin and GT Recycling are customer through our corporate gifting with GT Recycling being a repeat customer. We have massive growth opportunities with 2,422,404 active Australian businesses we can potentially target

Products: We manufacture the 57mm standard golf tee and 80mm wedge golf tee. The Naiteev ™ golf tees are the ONLY Australian made golf tee and the only golf tee made from Australian plastic waste. Our eco packaging and price point stand us our from our competitors 
Our customers are saying “these tees are durable with great performance on the golf course”. Being made from local car bumper bar waste is our point of difference
Our advanced manufacturing technology is strategically modelled so it can be replicated, and new products can be developed such as the plastic golf sand bucket or plastic car floor mats. Our manufacturing services and products can be re-established nationally and globally

Competitors: Three golf tees in the global marketplace are made from recycled plastic; The Boxhead Tee, Tour Tee, EcoGolf Tee The
Boxhead Tee is cheaper than the Tour Tee Boxhead packaging is eco friendly unlike Tour Tee and EcoGolf Tee
Our trial customers are saying they loving that The Boxhead Tee is the only golf tee made in Australia is the only golf tee made from waste plastic car bumper bars is the only tee with not for profit status and "has great performance on the golf course"
Business Model: Our three target revenu areas
Scenario 1 Golfers Wholesale we target 2% of total Australian Participant (986,479) in 1 year equals 351200 standard tees and 175600 wedge tees 
Scenario 2 Corporate Gift Sales In month 1 we secured 2 businesses with $1071.89 in sales a further 2 new businesses a month Totalling 4800 tees
Scenario 3 Trade Sales We aim for 30 pro shops in the first year Our research shows golf pro shops sell 70 packets of tees a month. We would aim for 10% of those sales equating to 18900 tees
We attract sales via our online store with a 250% profit margin and have a local stockist My Green Gadget
